title: QA Control Stack Report — cross-domain orbit equivalence: cymatics and seismology
_fixture_purpose: Canonical PASS report for the control stack. Packages [117] for external readers with: theorem statement; one-line summaries of [106], [105], [110]; a two-row comparison table; canonical PASS and FAIL witnesses; source refs. Validator checks that all comparison table rows are cross-domain consistent (orbit_path and path_length_k uniform) and that the report accurately represents [117].
theorem_statement: The QA plan-control compiler law (QA_PLAN_CONTROL_COMPILER_CERT.v1, [106]) is domain-generic: the orbit trajectory singularity→satellite→cosmos and path_length_k=2 are structural invariants, preserved across distinct physical domains under the same kernel-governed generator algebra. Cymatics (Faraday instability, flat→stripes→hexagons) and seismology (elastic wave propagation, quiet→p_wave→surface_wave) share the same QA orbit trajectory despite using entirely different physical state labels and move implementations. The compiler law governs abstract orbit structure; the physical domain provides only concrete instantiation.

domain_summaries.one_line: The kernel-governed compilation relation that maps any domain's planner cert to a control cert, preserving orbit trajectory and path length as abstract invariants.
Faraday instability: flat (singularity) → stripes (satellite) → hexagons (cosmos) via increase_amplitude + set_frequency; path_length_k=2.
Elastic wave propagation: quiet (singularity) → p_wave (satellite) → surface_wave (cosmos) via increase_gain + apply_lowpass; path_length_k=2.

comparison_table.notes: Faraday instability (fluid). flat=sub-threshold, stripes=symmetry-breaking threshold, hexagons=organized hexagonal pattern.
Elastic wave propagation (solid). quiet=quiescent, p_wave=compressional primary wave, surface_wave=organized long-period propagation.

canonical_pass_witness.description: Both domains instantiate orbit_path=[singularity,satellite,cosmos] with path_length_k=2 under different physical generators. cross_domain_claim: orbit_trajectory_preserved=true, path_length_equal=true, compiler_law_domain_generic=true.

canonical_fail_witness.description: Seismology trace declares final_orbit_family=satellite instead of cosmos, giving orbit_path=[singularity,satellite,satellite]. Trajectories disagree → ORBIT_TRAJECTORY_MISMATCH. Cross_domain_claim still asserts orbit_trajectory_preserved=true → CROSS_DOMAIN_CLAIM_INCONSISTENT. compiler_law_domain_generic=true with broken orbit → STACK_INCONSISTENCY.

validation_checks.description: domain_summaries covers [106], [105], [110] (≥3 entries, all required schemas)
all comparison_table rows have consistent orbit_path
all comparison_table rows have equal path_length_k
